channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Musk team’s access to student loan systems raises alarm over borrowers' personal information
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats are pushing back against Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency as it turns its attention to the Education Department, with lawmakers raising concerns about DOGE’s access to internal systems containing personal information on tens of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate moves full steam ahead on massive Trump budget bill after GOP divisions derail House
item.description: Senate Republicans are moving ahead with a bill to pass President Donald Trump's agenda on a range of issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris dodges questions about political future, possible run for California governor
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris is not giving the press any hints about her political future as speculations about a 2026 gubernatorial bid or a 2028 presidential run swirl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats try to enter Department of Education amid outrage over possible DOGE cuts
item.description: Democrat lawmakers attempted to enter the Department of Education building in Washington, but were blocked by a security guard who did not detail why he would not let them enter.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red states pursue their own DOGE-style reforms
item.description: Numerous Republican-led states are seeking to implement measures to slash the size and cost of state governments in an effort to mirror the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal buyout deadline extended as Trump plans USAID cuts
item.description: Trump's plan to cut staffing for U.S. aid projects worldwide would leave fewer than 300 workers out of thousands.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Historic Black church given ‘Proud Boys’ trademark calls for stand against hate
item.description: A judge ordered the naming rights of the extremist group the Proud Boys be given to the Metropolitan African Methodist Episcopal Church in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Judge in Boston to consider latest bid to block Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: A federal judge in Boston says he will take under advisement a request from 18 states to block President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Trump's federal worker buyout plan could impact San Diego
item.description: As the deadline looms for workers to accept the Trump administration's sweeping separation offer, the more than two million civilian employees by the federal government across the country are weighing whether to take it.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: More of Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Some are already at work.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Some federal health websites restored, others still down, after data purge
item.description: While some information has been restored, scientists are still alarmed over the removal of data. It’s not clear what has changed, and some pages remain offline.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Pien Huang, MPR News

item.title: Dems, GOP spar on schools as Education Department hangs in the balance
item.description: The House Education and the Workforce Committee's first hearing of the year saw Democrats take on the "elephant in the room" as President Trump and Republicans seek to move toward abolishing the Education Department, though GOP lawmakers largely declined to address the issue.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: 60K federal workers ready to take buyouts as judge blocks effort
item.description: So far, more than 60,000 employees, about 2% of the federal civilian workforce, have accepted the buyout.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Why CBS stands at the epicenter of Trump's assault on the media
item.description: CBS News appears likely to settle a lawsuit brought by President Trump in a case that legal observers say it should win. The network is caught up in Trump's broader assault on the media and an owner intent on finalizing a sale.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota House DFL, GOP strike agreement to end stalemate
item.description: A standoff that has stretched on for more than three weeks is ending in the Minnesota House. A deal will get the chamber up and running.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: Trump OPM buyout offers leave federal workers with risky decisions
item.description: The Trump administration and federal employee unions are in a head-to-head lobbying battle as workers face a Feb. 6 deadline to decide whether they want to accept a government buyout.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Education a key focus for lawmakers, DeSantis in 2025 state budget
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis is urging lawmakers to consider his state budget proposal one month before they are back in Tallahassee to begin the 2025 legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stauber reintroduces bill to reverse mining ban near Boundary Waters, return leases to Twin Metals
item.description: Northern Minnesota Republican Congressman Pete Stauber has reintroduced legislation to overturn a 20-year mining ban near the Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness. If passed, the bill would also return mineral leases to Twin Metals, the company vying to build an underground copper mine near Ely.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: State lawmaker behind ‘trans refuge’ law blasts Trump order on trans athletes in school
item.description: Rep. Leigh Finke, DFL-St. Paul, says the order is saying that transgender people should not be allowed to be active members of society.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Trump picks Neil Jacobs to head NOAA, a scientist with 'Sharpiegate' ties
item.description: President Donald Trump has nominated Neil Jacobs to head NOAA. Some former NOAA employees are concerned about Jacobs' scientific integrity after his prior involvement in “Sharpiegate.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda and Julia Simon, MPR News

item.title: Race for NYC mayor heats up as primary opponents hit Adams on education, immigrants
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (PIX11) -- During a budget hearing in front of the state legislature Tuesday, two Democrats looking to unseat fellow Democratic Mayor Eric Adams took their shot. State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11
